threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' with a capital T when you mean the Overground, and you're baffled by the 41 bus going the wrong way.
Saw a guy on the 3 bus last night trying to sell a goldfish in a plastic bag. Shouted ‘Brixton’s finest!’ as he got off at Coldharbour Lane.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a stray cat guarding a kebab shop during a midnight rain. Felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can stumble upon impromptu street performances in the U-Bahn stations, like a saxophonist playing under the lights of Görlitzer Bahnhof—gives the morning commute a unexpected charm.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they shrug off the random BART delays, like it's part of their daily cardio routine. Tourists gasp, locals just sigh.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a guy in a tuxedo chasing after a Muni bus that just left. Only in SF would you dress up for a gala and still miss your ride.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the Gràcia plazas are for lounging. They don't see locals using them as a stage for impromptu concerts and family gatherings.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their bewildered faces staring at the metro map, still clutching a paper map instead of using their phones like everyone else.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus stops and everyone suddenly jumps off even if it’s not their stop. Just wait, you'll get the hang of it.
Last night on the 59, a bloke serenaded us with old Motown hits. Only in Brixton would you find an impromptu karaoke session at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade on the corner of Weserstraße, while his mom chatted with the neighbors. That’s when it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 3 AM after clubbing, then ice cream from a shop that takes credit cards but not cash. Classic Berlin chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone is local when they can spot a tourist by their confused face when a bus is late for the third time that week.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 last week near Civic Center, the driver had to dodge a guy in a full dinosaur suit. Just another day in the city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often assume Gràcia's plazas are for chatting, but most locals just grab a quick coffee and dash off. It's not a lounge, it's a pit stop.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro map while everyone else seamlessly weaves underground. It's that look of bewilderment that gives them away.
